title: k-th smallest distinct value in each sliding window (BIT over the value domain)
source: For each width-w window, the k-th smallest distinct value (1-indexed), or None if fewer than k distinct values. Two coupled traps: an inclusive count boundary (exactly k distinct still yields a value) and a complexity gate (naive per-window rebuild is O(n*w) and times out; intended is a sliding multiset + Fenwick prefix walk). Ground truth: independent O(n*w) brute force on small inputs.
